conditionDescriptions: Chronic kidney disease with severely decreased glomerular filtration rate
contraindications.clinicalRationale: SGLT2 inhibitors should not be initiated when kidney function is severely compromised as efficacy is reduced and risk of adverse effects may be increased
contraindications.riskDescription: Reduced efficacy and potential increased risk of adverse effects
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider other glucose-lowering medications such as GLP-1 receptor agonist, DPP-4 inhibitor, insulin, sulfonylurea, TZD, or alpha-glucosidase inhibitor
contraindications.sourceSnippet: SGLT2 inhibitor: Do not initiate when eGFR <20 mL/min/1.73m² (Figure 23)

contraindications.clinicalRationale: SGLT2 inhibitors work by blocking glucose reabsorption in the kidneys, which is not relevant in patients on dialysis who have minimal kidney function
contraindications.riskDescription: No therapeutic benefit and potential for adverse effects
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Consider other glucose-lowering medications such as insulin, sulfonylurea, or other agents appropriate for dialysis patients
contraindications.sourceSnippet: SGLT2 inhibitor: Do not initiate when eGFR <20 ml/min per 1.73 m2; Discontinue when patient starts dialysis
